title: DCPS 5V Triage — No Power / Low Power at 5V USB-C
WARNING: A2338 is MBP 13" M1. No MagSafe. USB-C only. Has DFR + fan. Different PMU from MBA M2.
description: Diagnostic steps when board draws no or abnormal current at initial 5V USB-C connection.

steps.name: Check USB-C connector and CC lines
steps.action: Inspect both USB-C connectors (page 59). Check for bent pins, corrosion, liquid damage.
steps.test: Diode mode on CC1/CC2 pins of each port
steps.if_fail: Damaged connector → replace connector. If both ports fail, suspect SoC or CD3217 damage.

steps.name: Check CD3217 port controllers
steps.action: Verify UF400 (ATC0, left rear) and UF500 (ATC1, left front) are receiving power.
test: Check PP3V3_S2_UPC rail at CF400/CF500 caps
steps.expected: PP3V3_S2_UPC = 3.3V, PP1V25_S2 = 1.25V (both need PMU running first)
steps.if_fail: If no power to CD3217 at all → check upstream: PPBUS_AON, then PP3V8_AON, then PMU rails

steps.name: Check PPOCIN_USBC_AON path
steps.action: Verify the USB-C VBUS → charger input path.
test: Check FF201 fuse continuity (page 55 HV POWER ALIASES)
steps.if_fail: FF201 blown → replace fuse. If intact but no voltage → CD3217 internal MOSFET not passing VBUS.

steps.name: Check charger IC U5200
steps.action: Verify U5200 (Suona B379, 353S01525) is receiving input and producing PPBUS_AON.
test: Check input at U5200 from PPOCIN path
steps.if_fail: U5200 not switching → check charger gate drivers (PP5231-PP5228 area, page 22). Check SYS_DETECT (J5150 battery connector).

steps.name: Check SYS_DETECT circuit
steps.action: Battery connector J5150 has SYS_DETECT circuit that must be asserted for charger to work.
steps.expected: SYS_DETECT_L pulled low when battery detected
steps.if_fail: Battery flex not making contact, or J5150 solder joints cracked (flex is SOLDERED to MLB).
